steps.title: Spot the punctuation issue
steps.content: The main clause ends with “...collective defense,” followed immediately by a comma and “each NATO member ______ to defend the others if necessary.” The key issue is what grammatical structure can correctly follow a comma after an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the comma rule
steps.content:
A comma cannot join two independent clauses; it requires the second structure to be a non-finite form like a participial phrase. “Pledging” is a participle that creates a valid participial phrase. “Has pledged,” “pledges,” and “is pledging” are finite verbs that form independent clauses, which cannot be joined to the main clause with just a comma.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
